stem: A application enters the immigration pipeline. The probability of passing document verification is 87%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing background check is 92%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing interview is between 59% and 73% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing visa issuance is 95%. What is the overall probability that the application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.449 to 0.555 as the interview pass rate varies from 59% to 73%. Point estimate: 0.502.

solution_steps: The application must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the interview stage creates a 10.6 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the interview rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
